As for whether they can run on Linux systems, some of these malware types are platform-agnostic, meaning they can run on multiple operating systems, including Linux. However, others may be specifically designed to target Windows or other operating systems.

(In a Mushroom Island biome)

[Engineer]: "Alright, I've set up the simulation model for the refinery process. Let's see how the new pipeline configuration affects the overall efficiency... (notices a strange, glowing mushroom growing nearby) Wait, what's this? It looks like some kind of bioluminescent fungus. I wonder if it could be used to create a more efficient catalyst for our cracking process..."

(The glowing mushroom suddenly begins to emit a soft hum, and the engineer's simulation software starts to glitch)

[Engineer]: "What's going on here? Is this some kind of interference from the mushroom? (examines the simulation data) This can't be right... the simulation is showing a 20% increase in efficiency just from the presence of this fungus. I need to run some more tests to see if this is replicable..."
